concerns.text: Task T022 implements 'global permutation test... shuffle labels globally before outer CV loop'. This directly violates spec FR-004 which mandates: 'perform 1,000 permutations... *within* the nested cross-validation loop'. The task silently relaxes the statistical methodology required by the spec, changing the null hypothesis from model signal to split randomness.

Tasks T029-T030 implement FDR correction on 'Permutation Importance scores'. This violates spec FR-005 which mandates: 'apply a False Discovery Rate (FDR) correction to the p-values of all 30 feature coefficients'. The task shifts the statistical target from coefficient significance to feature importance ranking, failing to satisfy the explicit requirement in FR-005 and SC-002.

Task T033 implements a 'regularization sensitivity analysis' (sweeping α). While the plan justifies this as 'dual analysis', spec FR-007 explicitly mandates only a 'sensitivity analysis on the median-split threshold... sweeping the cutoff by ±0.05°C and ±0.1°C'. Introducing an unrequested regularization sweep constitutes scope creep and a silent drift from the spec's defined acceptance criteria.

Task T025 implements logic to 'increase max iterations' but fails to implement the spec's Edge Case requirement: 'increase the maximum iterations to [deferred]'. The task omits the specific '[deferred]' placeholder mechanism required by the spec's edge case handling, potentially hardcoding a value or leaving the mechanism ambiguous.
